checks.name: RDF Validity
checks.description: Apache Jena RIOT parse with strict + checking enabled (parity with `riot --validate`). Pass = parses cleanly with no diagnostics; fail = parses but riot reports lexical / IRI / language-tag / datatype issues; error = unparseable.

checks.name: OWL Consistency
checks.description: Arachne reasoning over the model imported alongside go-lego. Inconsistency surfaces as individuals typed owl:Nothing.

checks.name: GPAD Compatibility
checks.description: Categorical: does this model fit GPAD's flat triple shape? 'Causal model' is not a defect — modern GO-CAMs intentionally encode causal flow that GPAD cannot represent. Backed by ShEx validation against gpad-shapes.shex.

checks.name: GO-CAM Shape
checks.description: ShEx validation against go-cam-shapes.shex — the structural contract every GO-CAM model is expected to satisfy (well-formed individuals, evidence on every annotated edge, references resolving to known GO/CHEBI/RO terms, etc.). Focus selection uses the SPARQL-style shape map in go-cam-shapes.shapeMap, evaluated against the model unioned with a pre-materialised GO `rdfs:subClassOf*` closure so class-membership tests can rely on a single-hop traversal.

checks.name: Disconnected Individuals
checks.description: Named individuals with no incoming or outgoing relations to other individuals (or via OWL axiom annotations).

checks.name: Multiply Reified Edges
checks.description: More than one OWL axiom annotation reifies the same (subject, property, object) triple.
